factors.name: Semiconductor selloff spreading
factors.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix tumbled >7% on Thursday. Chip rout spreading from Wall Street to Asia (KOSPI down 6%, Nikkei -2.5%) as Meta's cloud capex efficiency gains and Apple's moves to buy Chinese chips signal potential oversupply in AI memory/hardware. JPMorgan warned of dot-com parallels between hardware suppliers and hyperscaler capex spenders.

factors.name: AI capex sustainability concerns
factors.summary: Meta's plan to monetize AI infrastructure and reports of excess AI capacity are raising questions about the pace and profitability of trillion-dollar capex cycles. CoreWeave and Nebius shares tumbled as neocloud business models face competition from Meta's internal build-out.

factors.name: Record foreign outflows from Asia
factors.summary: $137 billion in record outflows from Asia equities as index concentration risk forces rebalancing. South Korea and Taiwan hit hardest as AI-heavy portfolios undergo forced diversification, signaling profit-taking in richly-valued tech.

factors.name: Fed guidance shift toward ambiguity
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's softer stance on rate hikes and coordinated retreat from forward guidance (Warsh, Lagarde, Bailey, BOC) dampened immediate hawkishness. However, this creates choppier price action ahead of meetings as markets lose explicit anchors—long-duration growth stocks face volatility risk.

factors.name: Real yields still sticky, nominal yields rising
factors.summary: Long-end Treasury yields climbing as investors price central banks holding rates higher for longer. Equity valuations remain sensitive to real rate moves; with sticky real yields and inflation concerns persisting (Korea inflation at 2.5-year high), multiple compression pressures remain on growth/tech.

factors.name: Oil decline easing inflation narrative
factors.summary: WTI fell below $68 and Brent to lowest since pre-Middle East war start as US-Iran talks show positive progress and Strait of Hormuz traffic resumes. Easing energy costs remove near-term inflation pressure, supporting case for stable/lower rates and reducing stagflation risk to equities.

factors.name: June NFP looming, expected sharp slowdown
factors.summary: June NFP expected to slow sharply to 110k vs May's 172k (per CBA preview). A weak print could accelerate rate-cut expectations and support growth equities; an upside surprise would prompt USD/JPY toward 165 and complicate Fed pivot narrative. Binary event hours away.

factors.name: Valuation scarcity amid multi-quarter rally
factors.summary: Equity market bargains now scarce as AI boom and profit cycle have already lifted stocks to fresh highs. Strategists warning valuations offer fewer bargains than earlier in the cycle, limiting upside room and increasing vulnerability to disappointment on earnings or guidance.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings season risk
factors.summary: No major earnings releases flagged in current news window, but AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA guidance on cloud capex, AI demand, and margin will be critical next 2-4 weeks. Any softness on AI ROI or capex cuts could accelerate selloff.

factors.name: Apple-China chip negotiations
factors.summary: Apple negotiating to buy memory chips from Pentagon-blacklisted Chinese suppliers (CXMT, YMTC), signaling severe chip shortages despite AI capex surge. Regulatory risk high; move underscores supply-chain fragility and potential for China export controls or US retaliatory measures.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is rolling over after a multi-month AI-driven rally as reality checks surface: semiconductor oversupply concerns, questioned capex ROI from major hyperscalers (Meta, Apple), and record foreign outflows from overconcentrated Asia equities signal peak euphoria. While the Fed's softer stance on hikes and oil's decline ease inflation/stagflation risks, equity valuations are stretched and vulnerable to earnings disappointments. The selloff has begun in earnest (KOSPI -6%, Nikkei -2.5%, US semis under pressure), marking the start of a potential multi-day unwind of AI excess.
catalysts_ahead: June NFP (expected ~110k, vs May 172k) — binary event; weak print bullish for rate cuts/equities, strong print extends Fed hold narrative
FOMC (July 16–17) — forward guidance on rate path; conflicting signals from Warsh create uncertainty
AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA earnings next 2–4 weeks — cloud capex guidance and AI demand commentary critical
China regulatory news — export controls on semiconductors, retaliation for US measures
Core PCE or CPI print if released in 72h — inflation surprise would reprice rate expectations sharply
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in strong (>150k) and Fed re-signals hawkishness or FOMC reprices rate hikes higher, downside could accelerate past 29000. Conversely, if mega-cap earnings beat and executives reaffirm AI capex optimism + NFP misses badly, risk-on reversal could squeeze shorts back to 30200+. June 6 NFP is a potential inflection point; conviction capped until released.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (NAS100 -0.69% in 24h, semis -7%) — do NOT chase lower yet. Wait for stabilization/bounce to 29900–30000 resistance before shorting, or wait for close below 29400 support to confirm breakdown into 29000. NFP release at end of US morning session (8:30 AM ET) is key pivot; do not take large positions ahead of it.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Chip selloff underway but not fully exhausted; wait for bounce to 30,000-30,100 resistance zone before adding short exposure, or watch for breakdown below 29,700 for continuation.

factors.name: Semiconductor Stock Rout
factors.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plunged >7% in Asia following US tech selloff. South Korea KOSPI dumped 6%, Japan Nikkei down >2.5% as chip concerns spread globally per Bloomberg. Core NDX semiconductor exposure under heavy pressure.

factors.name: AI Capex Sustainability Concerns
factors.summary: Meta reportedly planning to sell excess computing power, raising questions about AI capacity overbuilding per Bloomberg. JPMorgan flags dot-com era warning as AI hardware stocks diverge from actual spenders, echoing 2000 capex bubble dynamics.

factors.name: Apple China Supply Chain Risk
factors.summary: Apple in talks to buy memory chips from Pentagon-blacklisted Chinese suppliers (CXMT, YMTC), signaling severe AI-driven memory shortage but carrying material regulatory risk if formalized. Negative headline risk for AAPL weight in NDX.

factors.name: Foreign Outflows from Asia Tech
factors.summary: Record $137bn pulled from Asia stocks (Korea, Taiwan) as AI rally forces index rebalancing. Long-only funds trimming concentration winners rather than broad risk-off, but scale of outflow suggests profit-taking in semiconductor/tech leaders.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance - Dovish Shift
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh remarks dampened rate hike speculation, helping gold rebound and easing immediate rate-hike fears. EUR/USD and risk assets benefited from less hawkish tone, providing partial offset to tech-specific selling.

factors.name: NFP Data Risk Ahead
factors.summary: US June NFP expected at 110k (sharp slowdown from May's 172k surge) due Thursday. Upside surprise could revive Fed hike fears and pressure long-duration tech; weak print would support dovish case but market positioning uncertain into print.

factors.name: Treasury Yields / Real Rates
factors.summary: Long-end yields climbing across developed markets as investors price central banks holding rates higher for longer per Investinglive. Higher real rates compress growth stock multiples, particularly harmful to NDX's long-duration profile.

factors.name: Valuation Stretch Warning
factors.summary: UBS notes equity bargains growing scarce after best quarter in six years; valuations offer fewer opportunities than earlier in cycle. Strategists warning AI-driven rally may have extended too far, too fast despite bullish AI capex outlook to $1 trillion.

factors.name: Tech Sector Rotation
factors.summary: Jim Cramer noted market rotation away from mega-cap tech winners on Wednesday. Selling pressure concentrated in previous leaders (semiconductors, hyperscalers) as funds rebalance after strong Q2 performance.

factors.name: Oil/Energy Relief
factors.summary: Oil down to 4-month low (~$67.80 WTI) on easing Middle East tensions and Hormuz flows improving. Lower energy costs reduce input cost inflation and support consumer discretionary spending, modest positive for growth stocks.

factors.name: Dollar Direction
factors.summary: USD edging lower vs EUR on softer Fed stance, but PBOC set USD/CNY fix higher at 6.8088 (vs estimate 6.7929), signaling tolerance for weaker yuan. Mixed dollar signals offer no clear directional tailwind for NDX international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin reclaimed $60k on Warsh dovish remarks, suggesting some risk-on appetite remains. However, crypto upside not translating to tech stock buying, indicating sector-specific rather than broad risk sentiment driving flows.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 faces acute selling pressure as semiconductor rout spreads from US to Asia, with Samsung/SK Hynix down >7% and KOSPI -6%. Core issue is dual-edged: Meta's reported plan to monetize excess AI compute raises overcapacity concerns (echoing JPMorgan's dot-com warning), while record $137bn outflows from Asia tech reflect forced rebalancing after concentration risk built up in AI winners. Apple's talks with blacklisted Chinese chip suppliers underscore supply desperation but adds regulatory headline risk. Partial offset from Warsh's dovish Fed tone and collapsing oil, but sector-specific technical damage dominates. NFP Thursday is key catalyst that could either deepen selloff (hot print = Fed hike fears) or trigger relief (weak print = dovish case). Move is Phase 2 – underway but not fully exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (June) - Thursday ~12:30 GMT, consensus 110k vs 172k prior
Bank of Korea policy decision July 16 (inflation at 2.5yr high may prompt hike)
Continued Meta cloud monetization headlines and AI capex sustainability debate
Potential Japanese FX intervention (USD/JPY near 162.50)
Q2 earnings season kickoff mid-July with mega-cap tech guidance on AI capex
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if NFP prints significantly weak (<80k), triggering Fed dovish repricing that overwhelms sector-specific tech concerns and drives 'bad news is good news' rally. Also invalidated if major hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) issues strong AI demand update or if semiconductor supply shortage headlines intensify (reversing overcapacity narrative). Reclaim and hold above 30,200 would signal buyers stepping in.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted >9% in early Thursday trading as chip rout spreads from Wall Street. Semiconductor weakness is a primary driver of NAS100 downside given heavy weighting of chip stocks (NVDA, TSMC exposure).

factors.name: Tech Selloff vs Broader Resilience
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank noted S&P 500 slipped 0.22% as chip stocks sold off sharply, while equal-weighted S&P rose 0.24% to new record. This divergence suggests mega-cap tech underperformance; NAS100 is heavily weighted to mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Japanese Yen Intervention Risk / USD Weakness
factors.summary: Japan shifted to 'ambush intervention' tactics against yen shorts; USD/JPY fell sharply from 40-year highs toward 162.40. Weaker USD typically pressures US equities (especially exporters in tech). Fed rate expectations may be repriced lower.

factors.name: Fed Dovish Tone (Warsh Comments)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh indicated price risks have come down, goal to bring inflation back to 2% target, and expectation to hold rates through 2026. Lower real yield expectations typically support long-duration growth assets like tech, but market still digesting.

factors.name: US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) — Major Event Today
factors.summary: NFP report due 12:30 GMT on Thursday (today). Consensus expects June NFP to slow sharply to 110k vs May's 172k surge. High-impact event could either shock market toward higher or lower rate expectations; adds binary risk into close.

factors.name: Oil Prices Down 4th Week / Geopolitical De-Escalation
factors.summary: Oil fell >1% on progress in US-Iran talks; WTI dipped below $68. Lower oil supports lower inflation narrative and reduced stagflation risk, positive for growth equities. However, chip sector weakness is offsetting this.

factors.name: Real Yields / Long-Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: Swiss inflation slowed to 0.5% YoY (first slowdown in 8 months), easing ECB rate pressure. Gold extended gains on weaker USD; real yields appear to be stabilizing. Long-duration tech is sensitive but no clear real-yield shock yet.

factors.name: Asian Market Weakness / Earnings Resets
factors.summary: Nikkei 225 down 2.33% at close; Kospi slammed by tech selloff. Foreign investors pulled record $137bn from Asia stocks as AI rally forces rebalancing. Risk-off rotation evident in equities broadly.

factors.name: AI Capex / Hyperscaler Optimism (Nvidia Deal Flow)
factors.summary: Nvidia offering revenue-sharing agreements for AI startups; Amazon designing custom AI chips; OpenAI proposing 5% stake to Trump administration. Underlying AI demand story intact, but semiconductor supply concerns (Samsung, SK Hynix weakness) are tempering sentiment.

factors.name: Antitrust / Regulatory Backdrop
factors.summary: OpenAI government stake discussions add political risk; Meta defending WhatsApp; broader regulatory scrutiny on mega-caps. No imminent DOJ action but backdrop remains hostile to mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Volatility and Options Positioning
factors.summary: VXN likely elevated on chip sector rout and NFP binary event today. FX option expiries layered EUR/USD 1.1350–1.1400; risk-off mode present but not panic levels.

factors.name: Macro Divergence: ECB Less Hawkish, BOJ More Hawkish
factors.summary: Eurozone inflation disappointing (fading ECB hike expectations), while BOJ expected to raise again by year-end. Mixed central bank backdrop supports neither strong bull nor sustained bear case.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in a technical and sentiment pullback driven primarily by semiconductor sector collapse (Samsung, SK Hynix >9% down) amid broader chip-rout contagion. While Fed dovish comments (Warsh) and lower oil prices support longer-term growth equities, the near-term tech unwind is outweighing bullish catalysts. Asian markets sold off sharply (Nikkei -2.33%), and foreign investor flows out of Asia stocks (-$137bn) signal risk-off rotation. The 24h decline of -0.69% is meaningful but not extended; today's US Nonfarm Payrolls report (due in hours) is a major binary event that could either stabilize or accelerate the selloff.
catalysts_ahead: US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) June data — 12:30 GMT today (Thursday) — expect 110k vs May's 172k; could shock market on rate path
FOMC decision implications; market pricing revised rate-hold narrative post-Warsh dovish tone
Semiconductor earnings and guidance reset (NVDA, TSMC, AMD updates expected in coming weeks)
Hyperscaler cloud capex commentary (AWS, Azure, GCP) — crucial for AI narrative validation
China-Taiwan tensions or chip export control escalation
CPI, PCE data for June (likely early-to-mid July) — inflation trajectory critical for Fed path
screening.risk_warning: Bias flips BULLISH if: (1) NFP beats consensus and markets re-price rate-hike odds higher (supporting real-yield stabilization), (2) semiconductor stocks stabilize and NVDA/foundries announce strong orders, (3) Fed signals faster rate cuts than expected. Bias flips NEUTRAL/bullish if chip selloff is just a healthy correction and AI capex momentum persists.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (chip rout underway); wait for retest of 29600–29700 support or NFP-driven consolidation before adding short exposure. If NFP surprises weak (supporting yen intervention theory), NAS100 could find bids intraday; avoid chasing short at current levels.

factors.name: Semiconductor weakness / Capacity glut fears
factors.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix tumbled >9% on Thursday as chip rout spreads; Meta's AI capacity glut announcement fueled sector selloff. Nasdaq 100 heavy weighted to semis (NVDA, AMD, QCOM).

factors.name: Fed dovish repricing (Warsh comments)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh struck dovish tone on inflation; market now pricing 0% probability of July hike, 29% for September (vs higher prior). Should be NQ-supportive long-term, but immediate risk-off sentiment dominates.

factors.name: Real yields and rate expectations
factors.summary: Treasury yields slipped ahead of NFP; USD weakened on dovish Fed narrative. Mixed signal—dovish is NQ-positive, but Friday NFP data is biggest 24h catalyst that could reprrice.

factors.name: US Jobs Report (NFP) — imminent catalyst
factors.summary: June NFP expected +115k (Dow Jones consensus), with World Cup potentially adding +40k boost per Goldman. Market awaiting 12:30 GMT release; high uncertainty into event means consolidation typical.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings risk / Antitrust headwinds
factors.summary: Google lost final $4.7B EU antitrust appeal (8-year fight); OpenAI facing U.S. regulatory scrutiny (5% stake offer to Trump admin). Regulatory overhang weighs on mega-cap confidence.

factors.name: AI narrative / Capex optimism vs. capacity glut
factors.summary: Robinhood CEO bullish on AI traders, Nvidia revenue-share program launched, but Meta's capacity glut fears and leveraged ETF AI bets (SK Hynix selloff) show sentiment fragility.

factors.name: Dollar weakness / JPY intervention
factors.summary: Sudden sharp JPY spike on suspected BoJ intervention (USD/JPY fell ~100 pips to 161.13); USD weakened broadly. Weaker USD typically bullish international revenues, but risk-off tone overrides.

factors.name: Crypto strength / Risk-on signal
factors.summary: Bitcoin surged above $61k as inflation fears soften; Metaplanet bought $170M more BTC. Crypto strength often correlates with NQ risk-on, though weakened by chip sector selloff today.

factors.name: Prior analyst bias (Haiku SELL 7/10, Sonnet BEARISH 7/10)
factors.summary: Previous two reads on July 2 were SELL (6-7/10 confidence). Today's further semiconductor weakness (+1 point down from 4h to 24h) supports continuation of bearish lean.

factors.name: Intraday momentum / Technical deterioration
factors.summary: NQ down -0.44% (4h) and -0.69% (24h); Nasdaq 100 futures down -0.80% during European session per latest data. Move not dramatically extended yet, but momentum negative into NFP.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 sold off overnight on semiconductor sector collapse (Samsung, SK Hynix -9%+), triggered by Meta's AI capex glut narrative and chip oversupply fears; this outweighed dovish Fed Chair Warsh comments and JPY intervention-driven risk-off. Regulatory headwinds (Google EU antitrust loss) add to mega-cap uncertainty. Markets are in consolidation mode ahead of Friday 12:30 GMT U.S. June Nonfarm Payrolls—the single largest catalyst for the session, with expectations for +115k (including +40k World Cup boost per Goldman). Prior analyst bias from early Thursday was BEARISH (SELL 6-7/10); today's weakness confirms that setup. Real yields and Fed policy remain dovish-supporting in the medium term, but chip rotation and capacity glut fears dominate near-term technicals.
catalysts_ahead: US June Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) — 12:30 GMT Friday (imminent, 4–5 hours), consensus +115k vs. prior +272k; unemployment expected 4.3%
Earnings season fallout: mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA) over next 2–4 weeks
FOMC meeting(s) — next major policy decision (timing TBD, likely mid-July); market pricing dovish hold
China-Taiwan geopolitical risks (Russia-Ukraine strikes ongoing)
Semiconductor inventory reports and hyperscaler capex guidance (AWS, Azure, GCP)
screening.risk_warning: NFP surprise to the upside (>150k) would reprrice Fed hikes higher, weaken NQ on real yields / multiple compression. Conversely, weak NFP (<100k) + soft unemployment could turbocharge dovish bets and trigger NQ reversal higher. Chip sector stabilization (positive commentary from NVDA/TSMC) would invalidate bearish semiconductor bias. JPY intervention could trigger broader risk-on sentiment (lower USD, equity flows). Meta capex clarity or positive AI-demand commentary could flip narrative. Monitor VXN and dealer gamma in NVDA/AAPL for technical support.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing short entries into NFP event (4–5 hours away); if NQ holds below 29,900 and breaks 29,700, watch for retest at 29,650 as potential short-cover bounce. Long entries only if NFP disappointment pushes market lower intraday, then reversal attempt: wait for close above 29,900 on NFP relief to confirm bullish flip. Current phase is pre-event consolidation with downside bias; conviction limited (5/10) until NFP data prints.
